definitions: (Southern regional intensive) very; to a great degree
(frequently plural) the interest possessed by law or custom in some intangible thing
(of the side of cloth or clothing) facing or intended to face outward
a turn toward the side of the body that is on the south when the person is facing east
an abstract idea of that which is due to a person or governmental body by law or tradition or nature; ; - Eleanor Roosevelt
an interjection expressing agreement
anything in accord with principles of justice
appropriate for a condition or purpose or occasion or a person's character, needs
being or located on or directed toward the side of the body to the east when facing north
completely
correct in opinion or judgment
exactly
free from error; especially conforming to fact or truth
having the axis perpendicular to the base
immediately
in accord with accepted standards of usage or procedure
in accordance with moral or social standards
in an accurate manner
in conformance with justice or law or morality
in or into a satisfactory condition
in the right manner
intended for the right hand
location near or direction toward the right side; i.e. the side to the south when a person or object faces east
make reparations or amends for
make right or correct
most suitable or right for a particular purpose
of or belonging to the political or intellectual right
precisely accurate
precisely, exactly
put in or restore to an upright position
regain an upright or proper position
socially right or correct
the hand that is on the right side of the body
the piece of ground in the outfield on the catcher's right
those who support political or social or economic conservatism; those who believe that things are better left unchanged
toward or on the right; also used figuratively
examples: Certain rights can never be granted to the government but must be kept in the hands of the people
They righted the sailboat that had capsized
a good time to plant tomatoes
a right is not something that somebody gives you; it is something that nobody can take away
be sure your shirt is right side out
do the right thing and confess
he feels he is in the right
he fell flop on his face
he fell right into the trap
he writes with his right hand but pitches with his left
hit him with quick rights to the body
it isn't right to leave the party without saying goodbye
right a wrongs done to the victims of the Holocaust
she is not suitable for the position
take a right at the corner
that boy is powerful big now
the flower had been correctly depicted by his son
the right bank of a river is the bank on your right side when you are facing downstream
the right man for the job
the right side of the cloth showed the pattern
the right way to open oysters
the time is ripe for great sociological changes
they are endowed by their Creator with certain unalienable Rights
they have a right nice place
what's the right word for this?

definitions: bring forth or yield
bring onto the market or release
bring out for display
cause to happen, occur or exist
come to have or undergo a change of (physical features and attributes)
create or manufacture a man-made product
cultivate by growing, often involving improvements by means of agricultural techniques
fresh fruits and vegetable grown for the market
examples: I got funny spots all over my body
The Bordeaux region produces great red wines
The accused brought forth a letter in court that he claims exonerates him
The company has been making toys for two centuries
The new law gave rise to many complaints
The proud father produced many pictures of his baby
The tree would not produce fruit
These chemicals produce a noxious vapor
They produce good ham in Parma
This procedure produces a curious effect
We produce more cars than we can sell
the new President must bring about a change in the health care system

definitions: (used with negation) having consequence
a problem
a vaguely specified concern
have weight; have import, carry weight
some situation or event that is thought about
that which has mass and occupies space
written works (especially in books or magazines)
examples: he always took some reading matter with him on the plane
he had been thinking about the subject for several years
he kept drifting off the topic
it is a matter for the police
it is none of your affair
physicists study both the nature of matter and the forces which govern it
they were friends and it was no matter who won the games

definitions: bring into existence
create by artistic means
create or manufacture a man-made product
invest with a new title, office, or rank
make or cause to be or to become
pursue a creative activity; be engaged in a creative activity
examples: He created a new movement in painting
The company has been making toys for two centuries
The company was created 25 years ago
We produce more cars than we can sell
make a mess in one's office

definitions: belonging to the distant past
not the same one or ones already mentioned or implied; - the White Queen
recently past
very unusual; different in character or quality from the normal or expected; - Lance Morrow
examples: a strange, other dimension...where his powers seemed to fail
any other person would tell the truth
hearing was good in his other ear
his other books are still in storage
she lived on the other side of the street from me
the construction of highways and other public works
then we looked at the other house

definitions: (computer science) a computer file that contains text (and possibly formatting instructions) using seven-bit ASCII characters
a written account of ownership or obligation
anything serving as a representation of a person's thinking by means of symbolic marks
record in detail
support or supply with references
writing that provides information (especially information of an official nature)
examples: The parents documented every step of their child's development

definitions: a central cohesive source of support and stability
a mechanical device that prevents a vessel from moving
a television reporter who coordinates a broadcast to which several correspondents contribute
fix firmly and stably
secure a vessel with an anchor
examples: he is the linchpin of this firm
the keystone of campaign reform was the ban on soft money

definitions: a component of production; something that goes into the production of output
a statement that expresses a personal opinion or belief or adds information
any stimulating information or event; acts to arouse action
enter (data or a program) into a computer
signal going into an electronic system
examples: from time to time she contributed a personal comment on his account

definitions: indicating high probability; in all likelihood
with ease (`easy' is sometimes used informally for `easily')
without question
examples: a mistake that could easily have ended in disaster
easily the best book she's written
he could equally well be trying to deceive us
you may well need your umbrella

definitions: divide into paragraphs, as of text
one of several distinct subdivisions of a text intended to separate ideas; the beginning is usually marked by a new indented line
write about in a paragraph
write paragraphs; work as a paragrapher
examples: All her friends were paragraphed in last Monday's paper

definitions: (of actions or states) slightly short of or not quite accomplished; all but
(of quantities) imprecise but fairly close to correct
all around or on all sides
in or to a reversed position or direction
in rotation or succession
in the area or vicinity
on the move
used of movement to or among many different places or in no particular direction
examples: 20 or so people were at the party
I was near exhausted by the run
I've had about all I can stand
There were trees growing all around
he needs advice from someone who's been around
the baby was almost asleep when the alarm sounded
the car all but ran her down
the whole town was astir over the incident
virtually all the parties signed the contract
waited around for the next flight
wandering about with no place to go
we meet about once a month

definitions: (of especially money) immediately available
apprehending and responding with speed and sensitivity
completely prepared or in condition for immediate action or use or progress
made suitable and available for immediate use
make ready or suitable or equip in advance for a particular purpose or for some use, event, etc
mentally disposed
poised for action
prepare for eating by applying heat
examples: Get the children ready for school!
I was fixing to leave town after I paid the hotel bill
can you make me an omelette?
fix breakfast for the guests, please
he seems to have ample ready money
he was ready to believe her
the bridge is ready to collapse
the soup will be ready in a minute
their guns were at the ready

definitions: (of a voice) faint
(of children and animals) young, immature
a garment size for a small person
have fine or very small constituent particles
limited in size or scope
limited or below average in number or quantity or magnitude or extent
low or inferior in station or quality
lowercase
made to seem smaller or less (especially in worth)
not large but sufficient in size or amount
on a small scale
slight or limited; especially in degree or intensity or scope
the slender part of the back
examples: a modest man of the people
a newspaper with a modest circulation
a series of death struggles with small time in between
e.e.cummings's poetry is written all in minuscule letters
helped in my own small way

what a big little boy you are

definitions: an indefinite or unknown location
in or at or to some place; ; (`someplace' is used informally for `somewhere')
examples: they moved to somewhere in Spain

definitions: by chance
examples: it may peradventure be thought that there never was such a time
we may possibly run into them at the concert

definitions: a basic knitting stitch
clearly revealed to the mind or the senses or judgment
express complaints, discontent, displeasure, or unhappiness
extensive tract of level open land
free from any effort to soften to disguise
lacking embellishment or ornamentation
lacking in physical beauty or proportion
lacking patterns especially in color
not elaborate or elaborated; simple
not mixed with extraneous elements
unmistakably (`plain' is often used informally for `plainly')
examples: I thought he owned the property, but apparently not
She has a lot to kick about

functional architecture featuring stark unornamented concrete

several of the buildings were downright homely
she has apparently been living here for some time

the unvarnished candor of old people and children

definitions: at all times; all the time and on every occasion
at any time or in any event
forever; throughout all time
without interruption
without variation or change, in every case
examples: I will always be there to help you
ever hoping to strike it rich
there is always some pollution in the air
you can always resign if you don't like it
you could always take a day off

definitions: a strong feeling of anxiety
be concerned with
be on the mind of
be worried, concerned, anxious, troubled, or uneasy
disturb the peace of mind of; afflict with mental agitation or distress
lacerate by biting
something or someone that causes anxiety; a source of unhappiness
touch or rub constantly
examples: I cannot sleep--my daughter's health is worrying me
I worry about the second Germanic consonant shift
New York traffic is a constant concern
The old man worried his beads
he wanted to die and end his troubles
his worry over the prospect of being fired
it is not work but worry that kills
